headline.purpose: Page-level h1 — a tank name, a morph name.

body.purpose: Paragraph text, table cells, form values.

label.purpose: Form labels, breadcrumbs, muted context lines.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: The one shadow tier in the system. Every card/panel at rest. Never intensified on hover.

motion.name: hover-brighten
motion.purpose: The only hover treatment on the primary button — a small brightness lift, no transform, no shadow change.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default call-to-action across the registry — 'Add', 'Save', 'Create tank', 'Log parameters'.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { padding: 0.6rem 1rem; background: #70d6ff; color: #072433; border: none; border-radius: 7px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.95rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0369a1; outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-stakes action beside a primary button — Cancel, Deactivate, Remove.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { padding: 0.6rem 1rem; background: transparent; color: #55636e; border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 7px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.95rem; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #0369a1; color: #0369a1; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0369a1; outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Standalone content container — the base surface for every panel in the registry.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.1rem 1.25rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(16,24,40,0.06), 0 1px 3px rgba(16,24,40,0.08); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text/number/select input used across every form in the registry.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 0.55rem 0.65rem; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 7px; color: #16202a; font-size: 0.95rem; } .ds-input:focus { outline: 2px solid #0369a1; outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Difficulty Pill
components.description: Care-difficulty escalation badge — the one place color carries meaning at a glance. Four variants: easy (lime) through expert (pink).
components.css: .ds-pill-difficulty { display: inline-block; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 999px; color: #072433; } .ds-pill-difficulty--easy { background: #e9ff70; } .ds-pill-difficulty--moderate { background: #ffd670; } .ds-pill-difficulty--difficult { background: #ff9770; } .ds-pill-difficulty--expert { background: #ff70a6; }

components.name: Neutral Pill
components.description: Informational, non-actionable tag — light/flow/growth-form, account type.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-block; font-size: 0.75rem; padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem; border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 999px; color: #55636e; }

components.name: Toggle Pill (Vote / Wishlist)
components.description: Stateful pill button — confirm-vote or wishlist toggle. At rest it's a neutral outlined pill; active state fills with its status color.
components.css: .ds-toggle-pill { margin: 0; padding: 0.1rem 0.6rem; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 400; background: transparent; color: #55636e; border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-toggle-pill--active { background: #e9ff70; color: #072433; border-color: #e9ff70; }

components.name: Tank Grid Cell
components.description: Signature component: one slot in a tank's grid. Occupied cells get the accent border and, if the specimen has a photo, a cropped thumbnail.
components.html: A1Frank
components.css: .ds-grid-cell { border: 1px solid #e1e8ee; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.4rem; min-height: 3.5rem; font-size: 0.75rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: space-between; gap: 0.2rem; background: #f5f9fc; } .ds-grid-cell--occupied { background: #f5f9fc; border-color: #70d6ff; } .ds-grid-cell__label { font-size: 0.65rem; color: #55636e; } .ds-grid-cell__thumb { width: 100%; height: 2.2rem; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 5px; } .ds-grid-cell__link { font-weight: 600; color: #0369a1; text-decoration: none; }

narrative.overview: This is a registry, not a storefront and not a field guide. Every coral morph, every tank, every specimen a hobbyist logs is an entry: a structured record with its own detail page, cross-linked to related entries, carrying real metadata the way IMDb carries cast/crew/ratings or Metacritic carries scores. The registering-and-maintaining act is the product, not a feature bolted onto a marketing site. This system explicitly rejects the generic AI-dashboard look: no gradient hero metrics, no cream/sand near-white neutrals dressed up as warmth, no side-stripe accent borders, no identical icon-heading-text card grids repeated for their own sake. Confidence comes from showing real numbers plainly, not from decorating the page around them.
keyCharacteristics: Registry-entry pages over marketing pages — dense, structured, cross-linked
One quiet accent color, used sparingly, never decoratively
Flat surfaces with a single whisper-soft shadow tier, not a layered elevation system
Coral photography and real data are the loudest things on any page — the UI recedes around them
One typeface, Roboto, carrying the whole hierarchy through size and weight, not font-pairing drama

rules.name: The One-Accent Rule
rules.body: Shallow Reef Blue is the only color used for a plain UI affordance. The four status colors never substitute for it — they only ever appear attached to their one specific meaning.

rules.name: The Ink-on-Accent Rule
rules.body: Anything placed on top of an accent- or status-colored surface uses Abyssal Ink, never white.

rules.name: The Single-Voice Rule
rules.body: No second typeface gets introduced for personality. If a heading needs more presence, it earns it through size or weight within Roboto's own range.

rules.name: The Whisper Rule
rules.body: One shadow value, used uniformly, never escalated. If a surface needs to feel more important than another, that's a typography or color-status decision, not a shadow decision.

dos: Do use Shallow Reef Blue for exactly one thing at a time on any given screen.
Do keep every card at 10px radius and the single Ambient shadow.
Do let hex values, vote counts, and parameter numbers appear as plain text at full contrast.
Do use Abyssal Ink, never white, as text on any accent- or status-colored surface.
donts: Don't introduce a cream/sand/parchment near-white background.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on any card, list item, or callout.
Don't use gradient text or gradient fills anywhere.
Don't add a second shadow tier or a hover-lift transform to cards.
Don't decorate the hex-color and element-color-key swatches as UI chrome — they render real sampled coral color.
Don't import a second typeface for personality.
